channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: In-sensor analog optoelectronic processing of concurrent event and memory signals for dynamic vision sensing
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 26 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68013-8

High-efficient dynamic vision requires capturing instantaneous motion and its temporal evolution to infer trajectories, velocities, and even predict future motion. Kim et al. introduce an analog event-memory sensor that is capable of event detection and temporal integration. The proposed architecture can reduce latency, power consumption, and circuit complexity compared to conventional dynamic vision sensors.
item.dc:title: In-sensor analog optoelectronic processing of concurrent event and memory signals for dynamic vision sensing
item.dc:creator: Yelim Kim
Hyeonsu Park
Minjoo Kim
Suhee Jang
Dae Yeop Jeong
Lia Saptini Handriani
Hyuncheol Yun
Namyoung Gwak
Nuri Oh
Sung Ik Yang
Soyeong Kwon
SungWoo Nam
Won Il Park
